channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Former Ohio officer to stand trial in 2020 shooting death of Andre Hill
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — A white former Ohio police officer is set to go on trial Monday in the killing of Andre Hill, a Black man who was shot as he emerged from a garage holding a cellphone nearly four years ago.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Some Jewish voters in presidential swing states reconsider their longtime devotion to Democrats
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — For Rona Kaufman, the signs are everywhere that more Jews feel abandoned by the Democratic Party and may vote for Republican Donald Trump. It's in her Facebook feed.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Anti-death penalty activists protest in Texas against capital punishment
item.description: Anti-death penalty advocates, including state abolitionists, former death row inmates and allies, held a rally in Texas calling for capital punishment to be abolished.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House says health insurance needs to fully cover condoms, other over-the-counter birth control
item.description: People with private health insurance would be able to pick up over-the-counter methods like condoms, the “morning after” pill and birth control pills for free under a new rule the White House proposed on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Seitz, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Dodgers finish off Mets with 10-5 win in NLCS and advance to face Yankees in World Series
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Shohei Ohtani carried the injury-riddled Los Angeles Dodgers to baseball's best record during the regular season. Tommy Edman joined the team in late July and helped them reach the World Series. It's been that kind of year for the big-spending Dodgers.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Texas man whose execution was halted by subpoena set to testify to lawmakers
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — A Texas man whose execution was halted after a last-ditch maneuver by lawmakers who believe Robert Roberson did not kill his 2-year-old daughter was expected to testify before a state House panel on Monday, four days after he had been scheduled to die by lethal injection.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How does abortion translate? Ballot measures are a challenge for interpreters
item.description: Reproductive rights measures are on the ballots in 10 states after heated debates over terminology and impact — and that’s just in English.
item.dc:creator: Michael Warren, Geoff Mulvihill And Philip Marcelo, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Should the minimum wage be lower for workers who get tipped? Two states are set to decide
item.description: Voters in Arizona and Massachusetts are set to decide whether employers should be able to continue to pay tipped workers such as servers and bartenders a lower minimum wage than non-tipped workers.
item.dc:creator: Kavish Harjai, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Judge will hear arguments to block Louisiana's Ten Commandments display requirement in schools
item.description: BATON ROUGE, La. (AP) — A federal judge on Monday will hear arguments on whether he should temporarily block a new Louisiana law requiring that the Ten Commandments be displayed in every public school classroom by Jan. 1.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Georgia islanders rushed to rescue survivors after dock walkway collapse that killed 7
item.description: There were life jackets bobbing in the water, people forming a human chain to pass survivors to safety and bodies being carried ashore in blankets.
item.dc:creator: Russ Bynum And Emily Wagster Pettus, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Oscar brings winds, heavy rains to eastern Cuba after striking the Bahamas
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — Tropical Storm Oscar brought heavy rains and winds to Cuba, an island already beleaguered by a massive power outage, late Sunday after brushing the Bahamas.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Calmer weather helps contain Oakland, California, fire that forced evacuations
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — Firefighting crews took advantage of calmer weather Sunday to increase containment of a small wildfire in Oakland, California, that burned two homes and forced more than 500 people to evacuate.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New Mexico authorities: 2 dead, 309 people rescued after severe flooding in and near Roswell
item.description: The New Mexico National Guard continued search and rescue operations Sunday in Roswell after record rainfall produced severe flooding in and around the city and Chaves County and left at least two people dead.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 2 Navy aviators declared dead after fighter jet crash in Washington state
item.description: The aviators' names won’t be released until a day after their next of kin have been notified, the Navy said in a statement Sunday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump works the drive-thru at Pennsylvania McDonald’s
item.description: Trump has been fixated for weeks on Kamala Harris's past experience working at McDonald's in college.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Kentucky AG investigating after man wakes up right before organ donation procedure
item.description: Kentucky's attorney general is looking into what the best possible action would be after an organ donation allegedly went terrible wrong nearly three years ago.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Singer among victims in couple's alleged Las Vegas theft spree during Super Bowl week
item.description: A married couple allegedly stole hundreds of thousands of dollars worth of valuables as Las Vegas hosted the Super Bowl, with one theft involving singer Jason Derulo.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KRON4

item.title: Romance scams on the rise as Americans look to dating apps for love: 5 tips to protect yourself
item.description: Almost 70,000 people fell victim to romance scams in 2022, according to the Federal Trade Commission. Experts told Fox News Digital how those dating online can protect themselves.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lane Bryant massacre: Criminal profiler says arrest could come in 2008 murder of 5 in women's clothing store
item.description: Criminal profiler says he believes unsolved Lane Bryant massacre can be solved with new technology and the public's help tracking down gunman who killed five.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Alec Baldwin returns to 'Saturday Night Live' to spoof Bret Baier interview with Kamala Harris
item.description: Alec Baldwin has returned to “Saturday Night Live.” But the actor who won an Emmy for playing former President Donald Trump didn't reprise that role — he returned Saturday night to play Fox News' Bret Baier in an opening skit that spoofed this week's interview with Kamala Harris.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: From showgirl feathers to shimmering chandeliers, casino kitsch finds new life
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Crystal chandeliers that once glimmered above a swanky lounge, bright blue costume feathers that cloaked shimmying showgirls, and fake palm trees that evoked a desert oasis are just some the artifacts making their way from the latest latest casino graveyards of Las Vegas into Sin Ci
item.dc:creator: RIO YAMAT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hurricane Oscar forms in the Caribbean
item.description: The formation of Oscar and Nadine comes as parts of the U.S. have been hit hard by a pair of hurricanes, Helene and Milton, that devastated communities, destroyed roads, caused power outages, took lives and injured many.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, ABC 4

item.title: Walmart to expand its new pet care business
item.description: Walmart Pet Services, which began as a one-store pilot project in the Atlanta area, will expand to five more locations in the coming weeks.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Olympian to drug lord: Snowboarder accused of running drug ring
item.description: Wedding competed for Canada in the 2002 Winter Olympics in Salt Lake City; according to the official Olympics website, he finished 24th in the parallel giant slalom event.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, NewsNation

item.title: CVS workers strike at 7 Southern California stores for better pay and health care
item.description: Workers at seven CVS pharmacies in Southern California have gone on strike for better pay and health care and to protest what they say is bad-faith contract bargaining by the company.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Oregon elections office shuts down phone lines amid false info surrounding Trump, voters' pamphlet
item.description: The Oregon Elections Division is shutting down its phone lines after the system was "overwhelmed" by out-of-state callers responding to false information about why Donald Trump is not in the Oregon Voters' Pamphlet.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Kustura, NewsNation

item.title: 6 Florida counties saw 'unusual increase' in flesh-eating bacteria after hurricanes
item.description: Florida health officials noted that six Tampa Bay area counties saw an "unusual increase" in cases of Vibrio vulnificus this year, attributed to floodwaters from back-to-back hurricanes.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, NewsNation

item.title: Utah court allows Australian student to play high school football after lawsuit filed
item.description: An international student who filed a discrimination lawsuit earlier this month has been allowed to play on his high school's football team.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, Fox 5

item.title: Some spots on Mars could have the potential to support life: Study
item.description: Dust-infused ice could create enough warmth below the surface to melt, giving life a chance, say researchers.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Mail carriers reach tentative contract with USPS that includes pay raises and air-conditioned trucks
item.description: Some 200,000 mail carriers have reached a tentative contract deal with the U.S. Postal Service that includes backdated pay raises and a promise to provide workers with air-conditioned trucks.
item.dc:creator: DAVID SHARP and NICK PERRY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Singer Zayn postpones US tour after the death of his former bandmate Liam Payne
item.description: English singer Zayn Malik on Saturday postponed a planned tour to the U.S. following the death of his former One Direction bandmate Liam Payne.
item.dc:creator: NICK PERRY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Indiana 3-year-old scratched by monkey inside Walmart
item.description: BEDFORD, Ind. (WXIN) — A 3-year-old is recovering after being scratched by a monkey inside a central Indiana Walmart store this week. The incident reportedly occurred on Wednesday afternoon inside the Walmart Supercenter in Bedford, Indiana. Local police responded to the incident.
item.dc:creator: Joe Schroeder, ABC 4

item.title: 3 killed and 8 injured by gunfire following a Mississippi school's football game
item.description: Three people were killed and eight others were injured in central Mississippi early Saturday when at least two people opened fire into a group of several hundred people who were celebrating a school's homecoming football win at an outdoor trail several hours after the game had ended, authorities sai
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The last in-person vote in the US will be cast on the desolate tundra of Alaska's Aleutian Islands
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — On a desolate slab of island tundra in western Alaska, a resident of Adak will again become the last American to cast an in-person ballot for president, continuing a 12-year tradition for the nation’s westernmost community.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: California woman slashed dog's throat, left it alive in trash: Sacramento deputies
item.description: The woman "felt her life would be easier if she only had to care for one dog and cut one of the dog’s throats," say officials.
item.dc:creator: Veronica Catlin, WGN 9

item.title: Buy a tiny house at Walmart? You can for under $16K
item.description: While there are dozens of things to consider before purchasing a tiny home, it's never been easier to find and buy one.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, Fox 5

item.title: Guardians starting Tanner Bibee in Game 5 against Yankees after short outing earlier in ALCS
item.description: Tanner Bibee is getting a second crack at the Yankees in the AL Championship Series. His first one didn't go so well. The right-hander will start for the Cleveland Guardians in Game 5 on Saturday after he was pulled from Game 2 in the second inning by manager Stephen Vogt.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, Fox 5
